title: Gymnosporia cassinoides
intro: Gymnosporia cassinoides, also known as the Cassin's spiny milk bush, is a plant species in the family Celastraceae, native to tropical Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Gymnosporia cassinoides include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Gymnosporia cassinoides is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, or used in combination with other herbs to make a tonic or syrup.
